questionHtml:
Daniela wins a prize. She is offered two options for receiving her winnings over a period of three years.

Option A: Daniela receives $5500 at the end of each month.

Option B: Daniela receives $2000 at the end of the first month. Each month after this, the amount she receives increases by 6%.

Sorin received an inheritance of $120 000. Sorin invested his inheritance in an account that pays a nominal annual interest rate of 4% per annum, compounded monthly. The interest is added on the last day of each month.

Find the total value of Daniela’s winnings and Sorin’s investment on the last day of the sixth month.
markschemeHtml:
Note: The first time an answer is not given to two decimal places in parts (a)(ii), (c)(i) or (d), the final A1 in that part is not awarded.

Sorin’s total = 1200001 + 4100 × 126 = 122420.09                    (A1)

Daniela’s total = 20001 - 1.0661 - 1.06= 13950.64                    (A1)

total value = $136370.73                    A1

[3 marks]

meta.title: Find the total value of Daniela’s winnings and Sorin’s investment on the last day of the sixth month.
breadcrumbLabels: SL 1.4—Financial apps – compound int, annual depreciation
